science.title: Giant underground neutrino detector brings scientists closer to cracking the neutrino puzzle
science.summary: Deep beneath the ground in China, the massive JUNO neutrino observatory has delivered its first major scientific breakthrough, achieving one of the most precise measurements yet of how elusive neutrinos change as they travel. Using just 59 days of data, researchers sharply improved measurements of key neutrino properti

science.title: Everyone thought these helmets were Roman until scientists uncovered the truth
science.summary: Researchers have solved a decades-old mystery by showing that a cache of 43 helmets found off the Spanish coast is medieval, not Roman. The remarkable discovery exposes a thriving weapons trade network that connected Mediterranean powers during a time of piracy, warfare, and growing demand for military equipment.

science.title: Scientists finally complete Schrödinger’s 100-year-old color theory
science.summary: Researchers have finally resolved a key problem in a 100-year-old theory of color, showing that the qualities we perceive in colors are intrinsic to the mathematics of color space itself. The discovery sharpens our understanding of human vision and could lead to more precise color technologies and visualizations.

science.title: Engineered bacterium turns potato starch into biodegradable plastic in 24 hours
science.summary: Every year, hundreds of millions of tons of petrochemical-based plastics are produced, much of which ends up in the environment or is incinerated. This exacerbates greenhouse gas emissions and the environmental crisis caused by plastic pollution. Now, a study led by the University of Barcelona has produced a biodegrada

science.title: The Strange “Spacetime Crystal” That Can Suddenly Turn Into a Black Hole
science.summary: A new mathematical breakthrough sheds light on how tiny black holes could emerge from critical states of spacetime. Black holes are often portrayed as cosmic giants, swallowing stars and shaping entire galaxies. But some of the most intriguing black holes predicted by physics could be far smaller than an atom. tech.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
tech.summary: Vulnerability in the Oracle-owned PeopleSoft software is about as critical as they come.

tech.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
tech.summary: Full autonomy is rare, but Ukraine is installing AI modules on drones and robots.

tech.title: Theker just raised $85M to build the factory robot that doesn’t specialize in anything
tech.summary: Unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form — think Boston Dynamics — Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured.

tech.title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world
tech.summary: The new round values the physical AI startup that aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design at $41 billion.

tech.title: Jeff Bezos’ AI startup aims to build an ‘artificial general engineer’
tech.summary: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos says his new AI startup will work toward developing an "artificial general engineer," according to reports from The New York Times and CNBC. The startup, called Prometheus, aims to develop AI-powered engineering tools to aid in the design of physical products. The NYT first reported on Prometh

medicine.title: Scientists finally crack an “undruggable” pancreatic cancer target and nearly double survival
medicine.summary: For decades, pancreatic cancer has been one of the most lethal cancers, with few effective treatment options. A new drug, daraxonrasib, targets the KRAS mutation that fuels most pancreatic tumors—something many scientists once thought couldn't be done. In a major clinical trial, the treatment nearly doubled survival fo

medicine.title: Traditional tertiary teaching models shortchanging neurodivergent students in health care studies
medicine.summary: Recent Deakin research into the experiences of neurodivergent students studying for future health care careers showed many experience stigma, inadequate help in classroom and clinical settings, and hard-to-navigate support systems. The study, led by Associate Professor Laura Gray from the Damion Drapac Center for Equit

medicine.title: Metal-free carbon monoxide prodrugs may help prevent cancer's deadly spread
medicine.summary: A carefully designed metal-free carbon monoxide prodrug—an inactive compound that is converted into its active form in the body—may help prevent some of the deadliest forms of cancer from spreading, according to researchers at Weill Cornell Medicine. Their recent preclinical study, published in Advanced Science, offers

medicine.title: Early heart changes may predict cancer years before diagnosis, long-term study suggests
medicine.summary: A new study led by UCLA Health physician-scientists suggests that subtle changes in heart structure and function may signal an increased risk of developing certain cancers years later. The findings, published in the Journal of the American Heart Association, could eventually help physicians identify patients who may be

medicine.title: Parkinson's medication shows promise in treating treatment-resistant depression
medicine.summary: For many people who suffer from depression, the condition is not just about feeling down but also about a loss of motivation and difficulty finding pleasure in activities they used to enjoy. A new study conducted in Sweden shows that a medicine used to treat Parkinson's disease can be used as an add-on therapy to allev

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics
mathematics.summary: Grothendieck is revered in the world of math; outside of it, he’s known for his unusual life, if he’s known at all. But what were his actual mathematical contributions? The post How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: Thruster breakthrough? New 2-in-1 propulsion system is about to get an in-space test
space.summary: A new propulsion system feeds the same "monopropellant" fuel into electrical and chemical thrusters, potentially saving on mass and complications for future missions to Mars.

space.title: I Am Artemis: Elkin Norena
space.summary: Listen to this audio excerpt from Elkin Norena, resident management officer, NASA’s Space Launch System Program: NASA’s Elkin Norena has helped the agency launch more than a dozen space shuttle missions – that’s more than a dozen crews to low Earth orbit and more than a dozen historic missions. space.title: SpaceX's stock wasn't the company's only launch today — It also put 29 Starlink satellites in orbit
space.summary: Liftoff of the Falcon 9 rocket occurred at 8:27 a.m. ET, just over an hour before SpaceX went public on the NASDAQ stock market.

space.title: Why is the US Space Force researching 'orbital warehouses'?
space.summary: As the US military readies for two 2027 missions to test spacecraft maneuvering, a new challenge with orbital warehouses aims to beef up space infrastructure.

space.title: NASA's ambitious Artemis 3 mission includes 3 giant rocket launches, 2 private moon landers and 1 big question: Can it all work together?
space.summary: NASA's Artemis 3 mission in 2027 looks to be one of agency's most complicated missions ever, with three giant rocket launches and two private moon lander prototypes that haven't been built yet.

astronomy.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers 'galaxy-killing' wind that may explain why some early galaxies lived fast and died young
astronomy.summary: A “galaxy-killing” wind driven by cosmic mergers may explain why many massive galaxies in the early universe stopped forming stars far earlier than expected, according to new JWST and ALMA observations.

astronomy.title: Did A Moon-Size Planet Grow Fast and Die Young in the Early Solar System?
astronomy.summary: A rare meteorite recovered from the Sahara Desert could be a fragment of a Moon-size body that met a violent end in the earliest days of the solar system. The post Did A Moon-Size Planet Grow Fast and Die Young in the Early Solar System? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 12 – 21
astronomy.summary: The three planets in the western twilight are pulling away from each other now. On Wednesday, the Moon will occult Venus in daylight. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 12 – 21 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Reading the Galaxy's Past
astronomy.summary: Every galaxy you've ever seen in a photograph is hiding something. Beyond the glowing disc of stars and gas that the camera captures lies a vast, ghostly outer region called a halo, too faint to see easily but packed with clues about how that galaxy came to be. astronomy.title: On The Hunt For Cosmic Dawn And The Universe’s Very First Stars
astronomy.summary: After decades of searches, cosmologists are within reach of finding cosmic dawn. A longtime observational cosmologist explains.

architecture.title: Jing'An Investment Center / Nikken Sekkei
architecture.summary: The JIC Jing'An Center is an urban regeneration project that integrates mixed-use functions (offices, retail, and residential) and is situated adjacent to the Nanjing West Road commercial area in Shanghai's Jing'An District. architecture.title: Heat as a Design Partner: Trees, Soil, and Wind Corridors as Cooling Infrastructure
architecture.summary: "By 2050, almost every child in the world — nearly 2.2 billion children — will be exposed to frequent heat waves." architecture.title: Why Information Continuity Matters in Contemporary Architecture
architecture.summary: Unlike many other activities that now take place entirely in digital environments, the final result of work in the architecture, engineering, and construction industry does not remain on a screen. architecture.title: Fondation Beyeler to Open Expanded Campus by Peter Zumthor in 2027
architecture.summary: The Fondation Beyeler in Riehen, near Basel, will begin opening its expanded campus to the public this autumn, with the full ensemble set to be accessible in January 2027. The project brings together the museum building designed by Renzo Piano Building Workshop and opened in 1997 with a series of new additions by Peter

architecture.title: Wadhal subverts suburban architecture for home in west London
architecture.summary: Architecture studio Wadhal has completed Wade House in London, using textured brickwork, pared-back fenestration and concealed guttering to put a subtly contemporary spin on typical suburban architecture. construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Kier awarded extension of £140M South West Water contract
construction.summary: Construction and infrastructure group Kier is set to continue delivering vital maintenance and improvements across South West Water’s operational region over the next two years, following an extension to its role on the Network Services Alliance (NSA). The post Kier awarded extension of £140M South West Water contract

construction.title: Google vows $50M for skilled trades training
construction.summary: Within a week of Meta investing $115 million, another major data center owner is looking to supercharge the construction education pipeline.

construction.title: Ex-Chicago Housing Authority director, builder accused of $4.8M kickback scheme
construction.summary: A former property and asset management official fraudulently awarded construction contracts at CHA properties in exchange for funds, a federal court indictment alleges.

ai.summary: arXiv:2606.12945v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-running LLM agents accumulate interaction histories far larger than any context window, forcing a standing decision: what to encode deeply, what to forget, and what to retrieve under a fixed memory budget.
